# aspect-ratio

<FeatureLinks mdn="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/aspect-ratio" caniuse="https://caniuse.com/?search=aspect-ratio" status="Snapshot pre-CR snapshot, publishable" label="Reference links" />

## Plain-language explanation

Let aspect ratio participate in layout without padding hacks.

## Representative syntax

```css
aspect-ratio: 16 / 9;
```

## Demo

<CssDemo title="aspect-ratio" css-b64="" code-b64="" html-b64="" caption="This example lets size relationships come from CSS constraints instead of a fixed pixel value." lang="en-US" badge="Practical demo" />

## When to use it

<div class="usage-grid">
<section>
  <h3>Best fit</h3>
  <p>Use aspect-ratio in complex layout when you need to express alignment, wrapping, and order as layout rules instead of extra wrapper markup. It usually gives the stylesheet a clearer job than extra classes, wrappers, or script.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Example</h3>
  <p>A settings page can align every nested control row while a responsive gallery chooses the right packing behavior.</p>
</section>
<section>
  <h3>Watch out</h3>
  <p>Layout features can change reading and focus order indirectly. Check the experience with keyboard navigation, not only screenshots.</p>
</section>
</div>

## Source metadata

- Category: Sizing, Units, Math, and Value Functions
- Maturity / source: Snapshot pre-CR snapshot, publishable
- Representative syntax: `aspect-ratio: 16 / 9;`
- Position in category: 2 / 17
